title: Lesson observations
journey: Lesson observations are a sensitive, judgement-heavy process — a leader watching a lesson, recording structured notes against an official framework, and deciding what (if anything) an observed teacher gets to see. This feature is Mosaic's earliest-stage one: still going through independent design review, with real open questions — including whether AI should ever suggest the official rating (the current working answer is no, that stays human-only). It's also built on a separate development track from the rest of Mosaic, so its pace won't always match the other features on this page.

subfeatures.name: Structured observation editor
subfeatures.note: Free-form notes structured against the selected inspection framework.

subfeatures.name: Guest / session-only mode
subfeatures.note: Usable without logging in first; saving permanently requires an account.

subfeatures.name: Saved, private observations
subfeatures.note: An observer's own saved records, kept confidential by default.

subfeatures.name: Observation dashboard
subfeatures.note: A signed-in view of an observer's saved observations.

subfeatures.name: PDF export
subfeatures.note: A downloadable report, including right-to-left language support.

subfeatures.name: Selective sharing with the observed teacher
subfeatures.note: The observed teacher sees something only when explicitly shared, and only the fields allowed.

subfeatures.name: AI-assisted rating suggestion
subfeatures.note: Still an open decision — the current working position is that the official rating stays human-only.

testing.fixtureNote: No dedicated fixture coverage yet — this feature is still in design review, ahead of any implementation.
